verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II Golgi-resident glycosyltransferase, but it has a well-documented dual localization: a fraction reaches the plasma membrane where it functions as a cell-surface lectin/receptor mediating cell-cell and cell-matrix adhesion (notably on sperm, where it binds ZP3, and on somatic cells in migration/adhesion). The long-isoform cytoplasmic tail directs PM targeting. The surface pool retains its type II TM anchor with the catalytic domain exposed extracellularly, making it accessible to extracellular binders.
